title: Hut 8 Reports Second Quarter 2024 Results
text: Hut 8 Q2 2024 Earnings Report: Total revenue reached $35.2 million, representing a 72% increase year-over-year. Net loss of $71.9 million, primarily driven by $71.8 million in digital asset fair value adjustments. Adjusted EBITDA declined to negative $57.5 million from positive $14.8 million. Bitcoin Operations: Mined 279 Bitcoin during the quarter (down from 740 in Q2 2023). Total self-mined Bitcoin holdings: 9,102 BTC valued at approximately $570.5 million. Cost per Bitcoin mined increased to $26,232 from $14,907 year-over-year. Operational Metrics: Digital Assets Mining segment achieved 46% gross margins, up from 34% previously. Energy costs decreased 21% from Q1 to Q2 2024, dropping to $0.032 per kilowatt-hour. Total energy capacity under management: 1,075 megawatts across 18 sites. Strategic Developments: CEO Asher Genoot stated despite the network halving, gross margins in Digital Assets Mining segment rose to 46%. Secured $150 million strategic investment from Coatue for AI infrastructure partnerships. New Texas Panhandle facility with 205 megawatts of available power capacity.
